rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH BEST VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, July 1, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiff JOSEPH BEST¿s motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SAMUEL LEAL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, July 1, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Kroeger Equipment and Supply Company¿s motion to transfer venue is denied because the motion was not filed within a reasonable time after Kroeger filed its answer on June 19, 2009. (Weil and Brown, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (Rutter Group 2009) §3:567). Everything that Mr. Morris reviewed to provide factual support for the motion occurred no later than January 12. 2010 and most occurred prior to August 7, 2009. Kroeger¿s change of counsel does not excuse the failure to file the motion within a reasonable time after Kroeger filed its answer. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROY DEHART et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, July 1, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issue 4 (punitive damages) and denied as to issues 1-3 (negligence, strict liability and loss of consortium. There are triable issues as to Mr. DeHart¿s exposure to Calidria and asbestos attributable to Union Carbide in Hamilton joint cement. Assuming without deciding that a sophisticated user defense applies to the employer of a plaintiff or a vendor/supplier of an employer of a plaintiff, Union Carbide has not shown by competent evidence that either Boise Cascade or Hamilton Materials knew or should have known of the hazards of asbestos that were known or should have been known by Union Carbide and thus, assuming without deciding the applicability of the defense, there are triable issues regarding the sophisticated user defense. Under the rationale of McCann v. Foster Wheeler (2010) 48 Cal. 4th 68, Washington law applies on the issue of availability of punitive damages and that law does not allow for punitive damages. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROY DEHART et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, July 1, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. DEFENDANT HILL BROTHERS CHEMICAL COMPANY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is off calendar at the request of the moving party. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROY DEHART et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, July 1, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Bayer Cropscience, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its initial burden of production on the issue of asbestos content and plaintiffs failed to create a trial fact on that issue. Defendant¿s D¿Amico objection to Mr. DeHart¿s declaration is sustained. =(220/HEK)
